growth_records.cite: Buchholz et al. Efficient dilution-to-extinction isolation of novel virus–host model systems for fastidious heterotrophic bacteria. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-00872-z. [PMC8163748]
growth_records.snippet: Our new SAR11 isolate from the Western English Channel, Pelagibacter sp. H2P3α...showed specific growth rates of ~0.10, ~0.45 and ~0.84 divisions per day at 10, 18 and 25 °C, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Paper reports specific growth rate as ~0.10 divisions/day at 10°C (approximate value, no error given). Converted divisions/day to mu (h^-1) via mu = ln2 × (divisions/day)/24 = 0.6931×0.10/24 = 0.00289 h^-1; doubling_time_h = 24/0.10 = 240 h. Isolate is a warm-water SAR11 ecotype (subclade 1a.3), 100% 16S identical to Pelagibacter bermudensis HTCC7211. Medium composition not detailed in-text (cites ref 80 for artificial seawater medium recipe).

growth_records.cite: Buchholz et al. Efficient dilution-to-extinction isolation of novel virus–host model systems for fastidious heterotrophic bacteria. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-00872-z. [PMC8163748]
growth_records.snippet: ...showed specific growth rates of ~0.10, ~0.45 and ~0.84 divisions per day at 10, 18 and 25 °C, respectively.
growth_records.notes: mu (h^-1) = ln2 × 0.45/24 = 0.01300; doubling_time_h = 24/0.45 = 53.33 h. Same isolate/medium as the 10°C entry, different temperature reported in same sentence.

growth_records.cite: Buchholz et al. Efficient dilution-to-extinction isolation of novel virus–host model systems for fastidious heterotrophic bacteria. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-00872-z. [PMC8163748]
growth_records.snippet: ...showed specific growth rates of ~0.10, ~0.45 and ~0.84 divisions per day at 10, 18 and 25 °C, respectively.
growth_records.notes: mu (h^-1) = ln2 × 0.84/24 = 0.02426; doubling_time_h = 24/0.84 = 28.57 h. Same isolate/medium as the 10°C and 18°C entries.
